title: No var declarations
guideline: Use const (or let when reassignment is required); var is forbidden.

rule.message: var is forbidden; use const or let

bad_example: var total = 1;

title: No console.* in application code
guideline: Use the shared logger module instead of console.*.

rule.message: console.* is forbidden; use the logger module

good_example:
import { logger } from "./logger";
logger.info("ready");
bad_example: console.log("ready");

title: No explicit any
guideline: Type everything explicitly; use unknown + narrowing instead of any.

rule.message: explicit any is forbidden; use unknown or a precise type

title: No @ts-ignore
guideline: Never silence the type checker with @ts-ignore; fix the type or use @ts-expect-error with a reason.

rule.message: @ts-ignore is forbidden; fix the type error properly

good_example: const port: number = Number(process.env.PORT ?? 3000);
bad_example:
// @ts-ignore
const port: number = process.env.PORT;

title: Functions are camelCase
guideline: Function names use camelCase.

rule.message: function names must be camelCase

title: Result union for fallible operations
guideline: Fallible functions return a discriminated Result union instead of throwing.

title: No enum — use an as const object with a derived type
guideline: Never declare enum (or const enum). Model finite value sets as a plain object literal frozen with `as const`, and derive the union type from it: `type T = typeof Obj[keyof typeof Obj]`. This keeps values erasable, tree-shakeable and structurally typed.

rule.message: enum is forbidden; use an `as const` object and derive the type: `type T = typeof Obj[keyof typeof Obj]`

title: as const object as an enum replacement (values + derived type + guard)
guideline: The full enum-replacement shape: an `as const` object holding the values, a same-named derived union type, and a type guard for narrowing unknown input.

title: No non-null assertion (!)
guideline: Never use the postfix `!` operator to silence nullability; narrow explicitly (if-check, ?? default, or a type guard) so the compiler proves the value exists.

rule.message: non-null assertion (!) is forbidden; narrow the type explicitly instead

bad_example:
const el = document.getElementById("app")!;
el.textContent = "ready";

title: Type aliases are PascalCase
guideline: Type alias names use PascalCase, matching interfaces and classes.

rule.message: type alias names must be PascalCase

good_example: type UserId = string;
bad_example: type user_id = string;

title: Interfaces are PascalCase
guideline: Interface names use PascalCase without an I- prefix.

rule.message: interface names must be PascalCase

title: No optional-property variant bags — use discriminated unions
guideline: Optional (?) marks data that is genuinely conditional. Never merge several variants into one interface full of optionals; key the variants on a discriminant field (a literal-union property) so the compiler infers exactly which data exists in which state. Flagged mechanically when a discriminant key already coexists with the optionals, or when usages fill the optional keys in disjoint groups.

rule.message: interface merges variants behind optional properties; split it into a discriminated union

title: Discriminated union view model (key-driven narrowing)
guideline: Model view data as a union keyed on a discriminant so each state declares exactly the data it needs, and narrowing on the key gives full inference at the use site.

title: No Record<string, V> over a closed key set — derive the key type
guideline: Use string keys only for genuinely open, dynamic maps. When the initializer enumerates every key as a literal, the key set is closed: derive the key type with `as const` + `keyof typeof`, or use an existing union type, so lookups and exhaustiveness are inferred by the compiler.

rule.message: string-keyed map annotation contradicts its closed literal-key initializer; derive the key type

title: No exclusive boolean flags — use a status union
guideline: Booleans are for independent yes/no facts. When several boolean flags are mutually exclusive (never true together, each true on its own somewhere), they encode states of one machine: replace them with a single literal-union status key so impossible combinations (isLoading && isError) cannot be represented. Flagged mechanically from usage evidence only.

rule.message: mutually exclusive boolean flags encode one state machine; replace them with a literal-union status key

title: No stringly-typed parameters over a closed value set
guideline: A parameter typed `string` whose call sites only ever pass a fixed set of literals is stringly-typed. Hold the values in an `as const` object and type the parameter with the union derived from it (`type T = typeof Obj[keyof typeof Obj]`, same shape as the enum replacement) so typos fail to compile, completions list the valid values, and call sites reference `Obj.Member` instead of raw strings. Flagged mechanically when every direct call in the file passes a string literal and at least two distinct values occur; exported functions and indirectly referenced functions are exempt (outside callers are invisible).

rule.message: string parameter is only ever called with a closed literal set; derive the union from an as const object

title: No repeated inline literal unions — derive one type from an as const object
guideline: A literal union written inline in several places (same member set, any order) has no single source of truth: adding a member means hunting down every copy. Hold the values in an `as const` object and derive the union from it (`type T = typeof Obj[keyof typeof Obj]`, same shape as the enum replacement) — the set then changes in one place, and use sites reference `Obj.Member` constants instead of scattering raw literals. Alias declarations themselves are never flagged; when an alias for the set already exists the fix is to use it.

rule.message: literal union is repeated inline; derive one union type from an as const object

title: Freeze never-mutated literal constants with as const
guideline: A module-level const initialized with a literal object (primitive values only) that the file never mutates is a lookup table: freeze it with `as const` so the compiler keeps the exact keys and literal values, enabling `keyof typeof` key unions and precise value types downstream. Flagged mechanically only when no reassignment, property write/delete, or Object.assign against the constant exists in the file.

rule.message: never-mutated literal constant; freeze it with `as const` to keep exact key and value types

title: Call with the as const member, not a raw literal
guideline: When a value is typed by a union derived from an `as const` object (`type T = typeof Obj[keyof typeof Obj]`), reference the object's member (`Obj.Member`) instead of retyping the raw literal — in call arguments (functions, arrow/function expressions, methods), variable initializers and reassignments, parameter defaults, returned expressions, typed object-literal properties, and elements of `T[]`/`Array<T>`/`Set<T>` alike: the value then has exactly one spelling, and changing or renaming it updates every use site through the object. String and number members both count. Flagged mechanically only when the whole evidence chain — object, derived alias, annotated use site, matching literal — is visible in the file.

rule.message: raw literal passed where the parameter is typed by an as const derived union; reference the object's member instead

title: No as type assertions (except as const)
guideline: Never override the checker with an `as` assertion — including double assertions (`as unknown as T`), which are just two stacked overrides. Prove the type instead: narrow with typeof/instanceof/in, use a type guard, or fix the type at its source. `as const` is the one exception: it narrows to the literal type rather than overriding the checker. If an assertion is truly unavoidable, make the debt explicit with `@ts-expect-error` plus a reason.

rule.message: `as` assertion is forbidden (except `as const`); prove the type with narrowing or a type guard

bad_example:
const el = document.querySelector("#app") as HTMLElement;
el.focus();

title: @ts-expect-error requires a reason
guideline: A bare @ts-expect-error hides why the checker is being silenced, so the suppression outlives its cause unnoticed. State the reason (ideally with an issue reference) on the same line; the comment must not end at the directive.

rule.message: @ts-expect-error requires a reason on the same line

good_example:
// @ts-expect-error TODO(#123): upstream types lag the runtime value
const x: number = compute();
bad_example:
// @ts-expect-error
const x: number = compute();

title: Classes are PascalCase
guideline: Class names use PascalCase, matching interfaces and type aliases.

rule.message: class names must be PascalCase

title: No nested ternaries
guideline: A ternary inside a ternary hides the branch precedence; use if/else or early returns so each branch reads on its own line.

rule.message: nested ternaries are forbidden; use if/else or an early return

title: No string concatenation with + — use template literals
guideline: When a string literal appears on either side of +, the expression is string building: use a template literal so the shape of the result is visible and every interpolation point is explicit.

rule.message: string concatenation with + is forbidden; use a template literal

title: Derived record keys (as const + keyof typeof)
guideline: Let the source object carry the truth: freeze it with `as const`, derive the key type with `keyof typeof`, and type dependent maps/params with the derived union so keys and values stay inferred end to end.
